questions.question: Explain the difference between a distributed-memory architecture and a shared-memory architecture. Provide an example of a scenario where each architecture would be suitable. (Assume you have two CPUs, each with 2 GB of memory, and two processes, P1 and P2, running on them.)

A parallel program running on a distributed-memory architecture is executing two processes, P1 and P2, on two separate machines. P1 needs to exchange data with P2. Describe the steps involved in data exchange and synchronization between these two processes when running on a distributed-memory architecture.

Consider a parallel program running on a shared-memory architecture. The program consists of multiple threads. Describe how you would implement synchronization among these threads, ensuring that each thread writes the result to a shared variable without overwriting previous results or causing thread interference.
